rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic carbothiocyanation of N-alkenylacrylamides with KSCN to give thiocyanato-4-pyrrolin-2-ones, either acridinium-catalyzed (conditions A) or self-photocatalyzed by the diene substrate/product (conditions B).
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of KSCN (or of the reduced photocatalyst) to regenerate the oxidized PC; the excited PC* (acridinium under A, or the substrate/product 1a/3a acting as self-PC under B) oxidizes SCN- to SCN radical, and the anode reoxidizes the reduced PC•- closing the cycle, with concomitant cathodic H+ reduction to H2.

key_intermediates.name: C-centered radical adduct I from SCN addition to acryloyl alkene
cyclized C-centered radical II (ring-closed via enaminic double bond)
cationic intermediate III after oxidative SET

electron_flow: Photoexcited PC (acridinium under A, or 3-aza-1,5-diene 1a/pyrrolinone 3a as self-PC under B) oxidizes SCN- to SCN•, generating PC•-; SCN• adds regioselectively to the acrylamide alkene to give radical I, which undergoes intramolecular radical cyclization onto the enamine to give radical II; II is oxidized (SET to PC* or anode) to cation III, which deprotonates to product 3a, and PC•- is reoxidized at the anode while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down under both conditions A and B; TEMPO-SCN adduct detected by ESI-HRMS, supporting SCN• intermediacy.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down; with NaSMs instead of KSCN, DPE-Ms adduct 6a was isolated (58% A, 62% B), supporting free radical S-centered intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: CuCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Electron scavenger; completely suppressed product formation, consistent with SET pathway.

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: SET scavenger; significant yield decrease (to 30% A, 41% B).

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: Electron scavenger; yield decreased (43% A, 24% B).

probe_reagents.name: thiocyanogen (SCN)2 / NCSSCN
probe_reagents.observation: Reacted with 1a to give 3a in only 44% (A/B), 9% (Mes-Acr+/blue LED), 8% (365 nm UVA); less reactive than KSCN/anodic conditions, suggesting NCSSCN is not the key productive intermediate.

notes: Two operative regimes are proposed: (A) Mes-Acr+ acts as photocatalyst at 455 nm with electrolysis; (B) PC-free, where 3-aza-1,5-diene 1a and product 3a self-photocatalyze under 365 nm UVA. Stern-Volmer shows KSCN quenches Mes-Acr+* (not 1a), while 1a* and 3a* are quenched by KSCN. Computed triplet energies (1a 50.6, 3a 54.3, NCSSCN 28.9 kcal/mol) suggest EnT to NCSSCN is exergonic, but chalcone dimerization control rules out EnT photocatalysis by 1a/3a, so 1a/3a operate as SET photocatalysts. Low quantum yields (Φ=0.009 A, 0.037 B) and on/off experiments argue against long radical chains. Alternative NCSSCN-mediated pathway considered but disfavored. H2 evolution at cathode confirmed.
